finding.summary: True peak is at -0.3 dBFS, leaving only 0.3 dB of headroom on a track with a +5.0 dB fader level and no FX chain or parent bus in view.
finding.probable_cause: Track volume is boosted to +5.0 dB (track.volume_db), pushing audio.true_peak_db to -0.3 dBFS with minimal headroom for downstream processing or intersample peaks.
finding.confidence_reason: Track volume and true peak are both directly measured; the linear relationship between fader and output level is verified with no FX in the chain to confound it.

evidence_refs.description: True peak at -0.3 dBFS — only 0.3 dB of headroom remaining.
Fader at +5.0 dB is the driver of the elevated output level.
Sample peak at -0.7 dBFS confirms near-full-scale levels.
0.05 — capture is nearly all signal, so level statistics are reliable.

proposal.reason: Preview changing track volume from 5.000 dB to 3.000 dB to test whether true_peak_db decreases.
